title: Use monday instance storage as a databaase
excerpt: I found out that the monday storage is an instance-level storage only. My question is, how can I possibly use it as a database?

posts.author: AndreiGueta (Andrei Paulo T. Gueta)
posts.cookedHtml: I found out that the monday storage is an instance-level storage only. My question is, how can I possibly use it as a database?

posts.author: Matias.Monday (Matias)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello there,

I replied to this question in the email thread you opened

Talk to you over there!

Cheers,
Matias

posts.author: andrewp (Andrzej P)
posts.cookedHtml: @Matias.Monday , could you also elaborate here? I need help sharing data via storage between different views of the same app. So far, it’s not possible. Any plans to change it?

posts.author: Matias.Monday (Matias)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello there,

As of today, the API storage works at an instance level.

In the future, we will probably add the ability to keep data in app basis rather than instance basis.

There is no ETA at the moment.

Cheers,
Matias
